title: Attitude
excerpt: Attitude describes how one creature currently regards another creature, which can affect negotiation, cooperation, and how some effects interpret relationship state.
markdown:
# Attitude

Attitude describes a creature's current social stance toward another creature or group. It is a relationship-state signal used to interpret trust, cooperation, openness to influence, and some rules that distinguish between friendly, indifferent, and hostile creatures.

## Relationship State

Attitude is not the same thing as faction, permanent allegiance, or formal alliance. It reflects how a creature is currently disposed toward another creature in the active situation.

## Common Attitude States

- [[friendly_attitude|Friendly]] creatures are inclined to cooperate or respond positively
- [[indifferent_attitude|Indifferent]] creatures are not strongly aligned either way
- [[hostile_attitude|Hostile]] creatures oppose, resist, or wish harm to the other creature

## Rules Use

Attitude matters when a rule depends on social stance, willingness to cooperate, or whether a creature can be treated as a suitable social target. A creature's attitude can change if the world state changes, if harm is dealt, if trust is earned, or if another effect changes the relationship.

## Separate From Ally And Enemy

Attitude overlaps with allied and hostile behavior, but it is not identical to those combat-facing relationship states. A creature can be socially friendly without being a full ally for every effect, and a creature can be an enemy in combat even if it is not actively hostile in every moment.
